sourceMovie.title: Runaway Jury
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Runaway Jury for the same reason it broke through in 2003: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Gary Fleder keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: L.A. Confidential
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Runaway Jury clicked for you, L.A. Confidential (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Runaway Jury?
faq.answer: Try L.A. Confidential, Primal Fear, Juror #2—each shares DNA with Runaway Jury while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Runaway Jury?
faq.answer: Runaway Jury (2003) is best described as Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Runaway Jury worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
